title: fix: repair window size in macOS
body:
When a program is running on macOS with an external monitor connected, the values obtained through 
`glfw.get_framebuffer_size(self.win)`
will be larger. 
It is more appropriate to use 
`glfw.get_window_size(self.win)`
to retrieve the logical size.

step_plan.summary: Change WindowView.get_framebuffer_size to return glfw.get_window_size(self.win) (logical window size) instead of glfw.get_framebuffer_size(self.win) to fix macOS external/HiDPI monitor sizing issues, and update the method's docstring/comment accordingly.

steps.operation: Replace GLFW framebuffer-size query with window-size query and update docstring
steps.reason: On macOS with external/HiDPI monitors glfw.get_framebuffer_size returns physical framebuffer pixels which are larger than logical window coordinates; callers across WindowView (projection setup, per-frame GL viewport, background blit sizing) expect a logical size. Swapping to glfw.get_window_size centralizes the change and provides logical (point-based) dimensions appropriate for macOS external-monitor setups.
steps.side_effects: Behavior change from physical framebuffer pixels to logical window size: projection math, GL.glViewport calls, and background blit destination sizing will use logical coordinates. This may reduce rendered pixel density on HiDPI displays (i.e., fewer physical pixels used). No function signature/API change is introduced, but some tests or visual expectations (especially those assuming full-resolution framebuffer sizes) may need updates. MesaView (off-screen) is not affected. Recommend running rendering tests and manual checks on macOS with and without external monitors and updating any docs/comments that describe the returned units.
